zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley於1218年4月4日出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊時，他的父親Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeley51歲，母親Joan de Somery28歲。他於1242年與Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard在英國英格蘭肯特爾哈姆 卡素結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1241年登記服兵役。他去世於1281年4月4日在他的家鄉享年63歲，埋葬在英國布里斯托聖 奧古斯丁 馮 雷也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley于1218年4月4日出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾貝克萊时，他的父亲Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeley51岁，母亲Joan de Somery28岁。他于1242年与Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard在英国英格兰肯特爾哈姆 卡素结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1241年登记服兵役。他去世于1281年4月4日在他的家乡享年63岁，埋葬在英国布里斯托聖 奧古斯丁 馮 雷也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Berkeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places so named, such as Berkeley in Gloucestershire, named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scottish Barclay .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of   Berkowitz  .

variants.name: Barclay
meanings: Scottish: habitational name of English origin, from any of several places so called, such as Berkley in Somerset or Berkeley in Gloucestershire, both named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The surname was brought to Scotland from southwestern England in the 12th century; Walter de Berchelai or Berkelai was Chamberlain of Scotland in 1165. It was long assumed that they were from the family that held Berkeley Castle in Gloucestershire, but the historian G. W. S. Barrow (1973, The Kingdom of the Scots) adduced evidence that they are more probably from Berkley in Somerset.

variants.name: Berkley
meanings: English: variant of   Berkeley  . Compare Scottish   Barclay  .

nameMeanings.name: Maurice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Late Latin name Mauricius, a derivative of Maurus (a byname meaning ‘Moor’, i.e. ‘dark, swarthy’), borne by, among others, an early Byzantine emperor ( c.539–602 ). It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was popular in the Middle English period, but was not widely adopted by the nobility and became rare in the 17th century. Between the mid-19th century and the 1940s, it was moderately popular but has since faded again. See also Morris .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley was born on 4 April 1218, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, his father, Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeley, was 51 and his mother, Joan de Somery, was 28. He married Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard in 1242, in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He registered for military service in 1241. He died on 4 April 1281, in his hometown, at the age of 63, and was buried in St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, England.

Il a épousé Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard en 1242, à Chilham Castle, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1241. Il est décédé le 4 avril 1281, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley am 4. April 1218 in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeley 51 und seine Mutter Joan de Somery 28. Er hat 1242 in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er hat sich 1241 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 4. April 1281 im Alter von 63 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, England begraben.

Lui sposato Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard nel 1242, nel Chilham Castle, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1241. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeleyが1218年4月4日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアバーケリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeleyは51歳，母Joan de Someryは28歳であった。1242年にイギリスイギリスケントチルハム カストルでIsabel Fitzroy FitzRichardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1241年に兵役登録をした。彼は1281年4月4日に故郷で63歳で死去し，イギリスブリストルスト アウグスティン ス レス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St. Augustine's Abbey, Bristol, Gloucest: 영국 브리스톨 스트 오구스틴 테 레스' 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley(이)가 1218년 4월 4일 영국 그루스스터실르 버켈리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeley(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Somery(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1242년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 치함 카스틀에서 Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1241년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1281년 4월 4일 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 브리스톨 스트 오구스틴 테 레스' 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley nasceu em 4 de abril de 1218, em Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeley, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Somery, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard em 1242, em Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1241. Ele faleceu em 4 de abril de 1281, em sua cidade natal, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Inglaterra.

ru.St. Augustine's Abbey, Bristol, Gloucest: ст аугустаин т лесс' чурч, бристол, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley родился 4 апреля 1218 г. в местности биркели, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeley было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Joan de Somery, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard в 1242 г. в местности чилхам кастл, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. В 1241 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 4 апреля 1281 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности ст аугустаин т лесс' чурч, бристол, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Maurice de Berkeley 5th feudal baron of Berkeley nació el 4 de abril de 1218, en Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Berkeley 4th Lord Of Berkeley, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Joan de Somery, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Isabel Fitzroy FitzRichard en 1242, en Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1241. Murió el 4 de abril de 1281, en su pueblo, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en St Augustine The Less' Church, Bristol, Inglaterra.
